labels.132: The feature activates on text where words or phrases are artificially split across the >>> and <<< markers mid-word or mid-concept.
labels.1551: Listing multiple related items or categories separated by commas within a series.
labels.2065: The pattern is references to "has been" or similar perfect tense constructions indicating past events or states.
labels.2066: Four-digit numbers or numerical sequences embedded within text.
labels.2067: The feature activates on proper nouns and named entities (people, organizations, shows, places).
labels.2079: The feature fires on proper nouns and business/organization names set off by surrounding text markers.
labels.2085: Parenthetical abbreviations or acronyms are introduced by their expanded forms immediately before them.
labels.2123: Temporal phrases indicating when recent events occurred (yesterday, last week/month, Tuesday, etc.).
labels.2580: Text immediately following section headers, titles, or introductory phrases that begin new content blocks.
labels.3095: Parenthetical or clarifying phrases enclosed in parentheses or dashes following a noun or concept.
labels.3588: Names or titles of individuals followed by biographical details or accomplishments.
labels.4107: Transitions between contrasting ideas or shifts from one concept to another.
labels.4124: Tokens between markers define or introduce what a noun phrase means or does.
labels.5165: Game mechanic descriptions with specific game systems (Magic cards, video game stats/items, talents).
labels.5688: The feature activates on prepositional phrases and descriptive modifiers that provide atmospheric or sensory detail about locations and objects.
labels.5755: Events and conferences where something notable happened or was announced.
labels.6148: Location names, organization names, or proper nouns that appear at the beginning of sentences or after colons.
labels.6232: Four-digit years between 2000 and 2020, typically appearing after dates or timestamps.
labels.6256: Text describing wedding-related services, expertise, or styling in professional contexts.
labels.6714: Possessive constructions where a person's name or reference is followed by an apostrophe and 's' to indicate ownership or association.
labels.7696: Large round numbers (in the hundreds to thousands) appearing in text, often representing quantities, measurements, or statistics.
labels.7747: Proper nouns and brand/organization names that are capitalized multi-word phrases.
labels.8322: Transitional phrases introducing explanatory content or navigation instructions within longer passages.
labels.8824: Transition words or phrases (however, but, nevertheless) introducing contrasting or additional information.
labels.8832: Dates in MM/DD/YYYY format being parsed or recognized as separate numeric components.
labels.9795: The feature activates on text where a phrase or clause is interrupted by a line break or content boundary, marking transitions between distinct thoughts or sections.
labels.9849: The feature fires on text fragments immediately following a clause or phrase that describes a problem, consequence, or contrasting condition.
labels.9851: The feature activates on years in the 2010s (2010-2013) within date expressions.
labels.9852: Phrases introducing topics, tips, or questions that follow immediately after the marked span.
labels.10755: Feature detects large monetary amounts in the range of tens of millions to hundreds of millions of dollars.
labels.10828: Calls to action or prompts encouraging user subscription, signup, or notification registration.
labels.10860: Superlative claims or descriptions emphasizing world records, rankings, or "most/largest/greatest" comparisons.
labels.11313: Time duration expressions (numbers with units like "years," "months," "weeks").
labels.11338: Conditional or hypothetical statements expressing what would happen or be possible under certain circumstances.
labels.11851: The feature activates on text describing two entities or concepts being paired, combined, or compared together.
labels.12318: The feature detects parallel verb phrases connected by "and" where both actions describe related leadership, development, or institutional activities.
labels.12350: The feature activates on parenthetical explanations or clarifications that provide additional context or instructions to the reader.
labels.12877: Claims of professional expertise or extensive experience in a specific field or skill area.
labels.13313: Transition phrases connecting two related topics or concepts in instructional/technical text.
labels.13334: Capitalized phrases or titles that introduce topics, often appearing before colons, "of," or descriptive content.
labels.14336: The feature activates on text spans that appear to be section headers, titles, or prominent labels separated by formatting or punctuation marks.
labels.14396: Transition from completed action or statement to introduction of contrasting or additional information.
labels.15491: Year citations in the format "19" or "20" followed by two digits, marking temporal reference points in text.
labels.15946: Transitions or temporal markers that establish a timeframe or shift in context within a sentence.
labels.16426: References to students or student-related activities and demographics in academic/university contexts.
labels.16503: Titles or headers introducing upcoming content with a colon or descriptive phrase following the activation window.
labels.17409: Text describing benefits, features, or characteristics following "and" or similar conjunctions in lists.
labels.17439: Tokens between markers introduce or reference a specific establishment, business, or proper noun that follows.
labels.17939: Words or phrases describing problems, deficiencies, or negative conditions that need to be addressed or corrected.
